title: Euchiton involucratus
taxonomy: Euchiton involucratus is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia and New Zealand. The classification of Euchiton involucratus is as follows: it is a member of the genus Euchiton, which is part of the tribe Gnaphalieae. The genus Euchiton is sometimes included in the genus Gnaphalium, but it is now widely recognized as distinct. Euchiton involucratus is commonly known as the hairy cudweed or New Zealand cudweed.

intro: Euchiton involucratus, also known as the New Zealand cineraria or tree daisy, is a small shrub native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of E. involucratus include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
